New Terminal Output:

root@rack-ge107-ya-20250517193277:/app# cat > find_repos.py << 'EOF'
> import requests
> import json
> import time
>
> def search_github_repo(paper_title):
>     # Remove common terms that might interfere with search
>     search_terms = paper_title.replace(':', '').replace('-', '')
>     query = f"{search_terms} in:name,description,readme"
>
>     # GitHub search API
>     url = "https://api.github.com/search/repositories"
>     params = {
>         "q": query,
>         "sort": "stars",
>         "order": "desc",
>         "per_page": 5
>     }
>
>     headers = {
>         "Accept": "application/vnd.github.v3+json"
>     }
>
>     try:
>         response = requests.get(url, params=params, headers=headers)
>         if response.status_code == 200:
>             data = response.json()
>             items = data.get('items', [])
>
>             # Look for the most relevant repository
>             for item in items:
>                 repo_name = item['name'].lower()
>                 repo_description = (item.get('description', '') or '').lower()
>                 paper_title_lower = paper_title.lower()
>
>                 # Check if repo name or description contains key terms from paper title
>                 if any(term in repo_name or term in repo_description
>                        for term in paper_title_lower.split() if len(term) > 3):
>                     return item['html_url']
>
>             # If no perfect match, return the top result
>             if items:
>                 return items[0]['html_url']
>
>         # Rate limiting - GitHub allows 10 requests per minute for unauthenticated requests
>         time.sleep(6)
>
>     except Exception as e:
>         print(f"Error searching for {paper_title}: {e}")
>         time.sleep(6)
>
>     return None
>
> def main():
>     # Read papers from the text file
>     papers = []
>     with open('paper.txt', 'r') as f:
>         lines = f.readlines()
>         for line in lines:
>             line = line.strip()
>             if line and '.' in line:
>                 parts = line.split('.', 1)
>                 if len(parts) == 2:
>                     paper_id = parts[0].strip()
>                     paper_title = parts[1].strip()
>                     papers.append((paper_id, paper_title))
>
>     # Search for repos and write results
>     with open('/app/result.jsonl', 'w') as f:
>         for paper_id, paper_title in papers:
>             print(f"Searching for: {paper_title}")
>             repo_url = search_github_repo(paper_title)
>
>             result = {
>                 "paper_id": paper_id,
>                 "official_code_url": repo_url or ""
>             }
>
>             f.write(json.dumps(result) + '\n')
>             print(f"Result for paper {paper_id}: {repo_url}")
>
>     print("\nResults saved to /app/result.jsonl")
>
> if __name__ == "__main__":
>     main()
> EOF
root@rack-ge107-ya-20250517193277:/app#
